clusters.canonicalTopic: agentic security for coding and tool use
clusters.synthesis: Security concerns around agent-driven code and tool use, including permissions, delegated access, harmful side effects, and safe remediation at scale.

clusters.canonicalTopic: environment and host selection for agents
clusters.synthesis: Choosing and operating the right sandbox, host, or environment for agent work, including isolation, persistence, and environment-centered workflows.
End-to-end delivery systems that turn requests into shipped software, including project-based execution and the full planning-to-production loop.
Using verification, grading, and control loops to judge work, catch failures, and keep iterative improvement tied to measurable outcomes.

clusters.canonicalTopic: context and memory substrate for agents
clusters.synthesis: The reusable substrate for agent context, including what is known, retrieved, learned, and tracked over different time horizons.
Designing systems and skills around evals, benchmarks, and output quality checks rather than only model capability.
Architectural patterns for agent systems, including layered design, abstraction boundaries, function-like programs, and event-sourced runtimes.
Using structured data models, ontologies, and provenance-aware representations to ground agent reasoning in the real system landscape.
Workflows that expose reasoning, surface unknowns, and combine independent research with a final judgment step.
Controlling local or open-model behavior through version choice, update timing, and easy onboarding.
Operating agents and harnesses at scale, including continuous cloud execution and feedback from users and products.
Turning implicit system knowledge into explicit written context for security analysis.
